---
title: "Projects and Safe Sharing"
subtitle: "Choose the correct output and an approved destination"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goal — 20 minutes

::: {.incremental}
- Recognize what `_quarto.yml` controls.
- Distinguish a website from a standalone report.
- Choose a sharing destination appropriate to the content.
:::

# Project boundary {.section-divider}

## Two projects in this workshop

| Location | Type | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Workshop root | Website | Read-only lessons |
| `student_work/` | Default | Learner files |

::: {.callout-important}
Do not edit the root `_quarto.yml` during class.
:::

# Sharing unit {.section-divider}

## Website

A rendered website normally depends on shared assets.

> Share or deploy the complete `_site/` directory.

## Standalone HTML report

For a single report whose resources are embedded:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
```

::: {.fragment}
Test the resulting file offline before relying on it for archival or email delivery.
:::

# Format-aware output {.section-divider}

## Interactive plus static fallback

```r
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  DT::datatable(my_data)
} else {
  knitr::kable(my_data)
}
```

For DOCX-specific logic:

```r
knitr::pandoc_to("docx")
```

# Public versus internal {.section-divider}

## Destination follows authorization

| Content | Destination |
|---|---|
| Approved public material | Approved public host |
| Internal material | Institutionally managed internal service |
| Controlled or protected data | Only an environment authorized for that data |

::: {.callout-warning}
Quarto Pub and GitHub Pages are public publishing options. A private external repository is not automatically approved for agency data.
:::

# End-of-day check {.section-divider}

## Before leaving

1. Render `student_work/my_report.qmd` from a restarted session.
2. Confirm the table, figure, cross-references, citation, and bibliography.
3. Save or export the learner workspace using the instructor's approved method.
4. Record one real project you will try next.
